description: Source-tree anchors for the openjd-rs internals koans. Each entry records the git state of a repo at the moment the docs were written. Use `git diff <commit>..HEAD` against each repo to see what's changed since the docs were last verified, and update the affected docs accordingly.

openjd-rs.primary_role: Rust implementation under study; majority of file:line references in the koans point here

key_lines_referenced_in_koans.default_library.rs:27: the only Mutex<HashMap> in the workspace (doc 13)
key_lines_referenced_in_koans.value.rs:127: ExprValue enum with typed list variants (doc 01)

openjd-rs.drift_warning: If the wrap dispatch in session.rs is refactored, doc 16's line ranges and the overlay function names will need re-verification. The constants block in doc 11 is the single most likely drift source — re-grep all MAX_*/DEFAULT_* constants on update.

openjd-sessions-for-python.commit_subject: feat: RFC008 implementation - support OnWrap actions.
openjd-sessions-for-python.primary_role: Python reference implementation. Used in docs 17 and 18 for the file-by-file mapping and feature-parity scorecard.
openjd-sessions-for-python.drift_warning: Doc 17's file-by-file table and doc 18's feature-parity scorecard will drift if the Python codebase adds modules or extensions. On update, re-walk __init__.py and CHANGELOG.md.

openjd-specifications.commit_subject: feat: RFC for Environment Wrap Actions
openjd-specifications.primary_role: Source RFCs (0005 expression language, 0006 function library, 0008 wrap actions). Referenced throughout the koans for spec citations.
openjd-specifications.drift_warning: RFC 0008 is in active revision (working tree dirty at anchor time). Doc 16 cites RFC 0008 sections by name (Wrap ordering, Cancelation behavior, etc.) — confirm those sections still exist and have the same semantic meaning before treating doc 16 as accurate.

how_to_update.step_1: For each repo: cd <path> && git fetch && git diff <anchored_commit>..HEAD -- <key_paths>
how_to_update.step_2: Identify which koans reference changed files (cross-reference with key_lines_referenced_in_koans and the docs' 'Read these in order' sections)
how_to_update.step_3: Re-verify any line numbers cited in the docs; line drift is the most common refresh need
how_to_update.step_4: Re-grep the constants in key_constants_to_recheck; values are quoted directly in docs 04 and 11
how_to_update.step_5: On RFC 0008 changes: re-read doc 16 in full; on EXPR changes: re-read docs 01-08, 15
how_to_update.step_6: Bump anchored_at and update each repo's commit/commit_date/commit_subject; update working_tree_clean if applicable
